name: meth-syntax
description: Syntax highlighting, folding, bracket matching, and linting for Methlang (.meth) — a typed language that compiles to x86-64 assembly. See README for links to the language docs.

meth.linter.compilerEnabled.description: Run the Methlang compiler on save for semantic diagnostics (types, scope, undefined symbols).
meth.linter.compilerEnabled.markdownDescription:
When enabled, the extension runs the `methlang` compiler **on save** and shows real compiler errors and warnings in the Problems panel. Regex-based checks still run while you type.

Requires a `methlang` binary (workspace `bin/` or `PATH`).

meth.linter.compilerPath.description: Path to the methlang executable. Empty: auto-detect workspace bin/, then PATH.
meth.linter.compilerPath.markdownDescription:
Path to the Methlang executable. Can be relative to the workspace folder or absolute.

If empty, the extension looks for `bin/methlang` or `bin/methlang.exe` (and a few fallbacks) under the workspace, then uses `methlang` / `methlang.exe` on your `PATH`.

See the repo `docs/compilation.md` for how to build or install the compiler.

meth.linter.stdlibPath.description: Optional --stdlib directory override for the linter invocation.
meth.linter.stdlibPath.markdownDescription:
If set, passed to the compiler as `--stdlib <dir>` when linting. Use when you maintain a custom standard library layout.

Leave empty to use the compiler’s default bundled stdlib resolution (see `docs/compilation.md`).
